subject: Joint Distributions
topic: §42 Sum of Two Independent Random Variables: Continuous Case
question: Let $X$ and $Y$ be two independent exponential random variables with common parameter $\lambda$. Compute $f_{X+Y}(a)$.
explanation: We have $f_X(a) = f_Y(a) = \begin{cases} \lambda e^{-\lambda a} & 0 \le a \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ If $a \ge 0$ then $f_{X+Y}(a) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f_X(a - y) f_Y(y)\,dy = \lambda^2 \int_0^a e^{-\lambda a}\,dy = a \lambda^2 e^{-\lambda a}$. If $a < 0$ then $f_{X+Y}(a) = 0$. Hence, $f_{X+Y}(a) = \begin{cases} a \lambda^2 e^{-\lambda a} & 0 \le a \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$
